text.title: Web Scraping Element Extraction Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every extraction module must satisfy plus run-the-checklist + skip-this-methodology. Validator runs against the produced row JSON.

rule.statement: Use $eval for single-value extraction; $$eval for multi-element extraction. Mismatching the API to cardinality (e.g. $eval against a list) silently returns only the first match or throws.
rule.rationale: $eval invokes the function with a single element; $$eval invokes it with the array of matched elements.

rule.statement: In new Playwright code prefer the locator API (`locator().textContent()`, `locator().allTextContents()`, `locator().count()`) over raw `$$eval`. The locator API retries and auto-waits.
rule.rationale: Auto-wait reduces flaky-extraction by an order of magnitude on dynamic pages.

rule.statement: Every extracted string passes through the normalizer at extraction time: trim, collapse whitespace runs, strip non-printable chars. Prices parse to float (handle currency symbol + thousands separator). Dates parse to ISO 8601.
rule.rationale: Downstream code must never guess whether a field is " $1,234.56 \n" or 1234.56.

rule.statement: Parse failure (unparseable price, unparseable date, empty after trim) returns NULL with reason logged to a side-stream. Never throw from the extraction function for parse-shape issues.
rule.rationale: A single malformed row should not crash a 10k-row scrape. Surface to side-stream; downstream stage decides.

rule.statement: Table extraction MUST declare row + column expected counts. If counts mismatch, extraction returns null + records the mismatch; it does not silently truncate.
rule.rationale: Truncated tables become "the data is missing the last column" bugs weeks later.

rule.statement: For each row to extract, walk: shape (single / many / table) → API (Playwright locator preferred, Puppeteer $eval/$$eval) → normalize → null-coerce on parse failure → persist. Skip none.
rule.rationale: Skipping the normalizer is the principal cause of downstream rework.

rule.statement: Do NOT use when (a) source has an SSR JSON endpoint — hit it directly, (b) output is full-page LLM-targeted Markdown — use Firecrawl/r.jina.ai, (c) one-off scrape — manual is cheaper, (d) DOM is randomized per request — load resilience first.
rule.rationale: DOM extraction overhead is wasted when a JSON endpoint or Markdown adapter exists.
